question.content: In triangle ABC, AB=AC and point D lies on side BC with D not equal to B or C. Rotate segment AD counterclockwise around A by alpha to get segment AE, and connect DE. For alpha=angle BAC=90 degrees and BD<CD, draw DG perpendicular to BC through D and let DG meet the extension of CA at G. Connect BG. Point F is the midpoint of DE, and point H is the midpoint of BG. Connect FH and CF. Express the quantitative relation between FH and CF. Answer format requirement: <expr>. Strictly forbidden: any extra text beyond this format.

step_1.content: Use the complex-plane method. Put A=i, B=-1, and C=1, so triangle ABC is an isosceles right triangle and every 90-degree rotation is multiplication by i.
step_1.key_point: Encode the 90-degree rotation by complex multiplication instead of using Cartesian coordinates for every point.

step_3.content: Write D=d with -1<d<1. The 90-degree rotation about A sends D to E by e=i+i(d-i)=1+(d+1)i. Since G lies on the extension of CA, whose line is y=1-x, and DG is vertical, g=d+(1-d)i.
step_3.key_point: Obtain E from the rotation formula and G from the line equation of CA.

step_5.key_point: Compare the two squared moduli directly from midpoint formulas.
